_about: Per-quest provenance overrides for the dashboard quest library. Keys are decimal quest numbers as strings. The frontend fetches this file on load and overlays each entry on the default CategoryID-derived classification produced by adaptQuest(). Schema documented below.

_schema.originLabel: Human-readable origin label shown in the modal 'Origin' field (e.g. 'Sega DC download (2001)', 'Fan-made by soulja224466').
_schema.sourceUrl: External URL to the canonical source (Ephinea wiki article, original pack thread, attribution page, commit) — rendered as a clickable link in the modal next to the origin label.
_schema.author: Optional. Author handle for genuinely fan-made quests.
_schema.year: Optional. Approximate release year for chronology context.
_schema.classify: One of 'original' | 'community' | 'custom'. Overrides the default CategoryID-based source classification. Set to 'original' for Sega-authored content that happens to live under CategoryID 21 (the 'Download' bucket) because that's the historical distribution channel.

_notes: Research basis: see commit attached to this file. The 9 entries below for q050/051/052/070/071/072/078/081/082 are all Sega-authored download quests from the DC/GC era — the 'Download' CategoryID is about distribution channel, not authorship.
Only q000 (soulja224466) and q253 (Matt Swift, newserv-bundled utility quest) are genuinely third-party-authored in the upstream newserv bundle.
When new community packs land via server/quests/download/, add entries here mapping quest_number → provenance so the dashboard attributes them correctly.
